comments.text: Hey, CBP isn't allowed to do any of the stuff it's doing in Minneapolis even within the confines of its extremely ridiculous "100 miles from any border" jurisdiction. It's 300 miles from Canada and 160 miles to Lake Superior. They're breaking the law standing around, let alone beating up protesters.

Notable that Minneapolis is the rare metro more than 100 miles from the border or the coast (Great Lakes count), an operating boundary that lets CBP operate normally far beyond what anyone reasonable considers the border.

A reform that might be possible in under 3 years is to (re)define CPB’s role. They are currently supposed to only act within 100 miles of a border and no matter how loosely you define that, Minneapolis is still more than 150 from Great Lakes.

The Border Patrol is legally allowed to operate only within 100 miles of a U.S. border, including coastlines. Last time I checked, Minneapolis was about 150 miles from Duluth on Lake Superior, and even farther from the Canadian border.

Surrounded by the Great Lakes, almost every place in Michigan is within 100 miles of a border - according to DHS guidelines. Despite that idiotic ruling, the majority of Michiganders treasure our special relationship with Canada, much like other states that share that peaceful border. #AbolishICE
I just travelled 2,000 miles from the tristate border up to the Great Lakes. They were not checking the buses nor stations. Just typing this for no reason.

US Border Patrol has NO jurisdiction in Charlotte NC - it's not within 100 miles of any border. And we need to change the Great Lakes bullshit that defines Chicago within 100 miles of a border. In fact, the entire "100 mile Border Zone" is not even a law - it's an arbitrary number from 1953.

Possibly because of the Customs and Border Patrol's jurisdiction to search and detain up to 100 miles inland from any U.S. border. Chicago is a disputed area because it is more than 100 miles from Canada, but the Great Lakes are recognized as international boundary waters. Regardless, CBP sucks.

JB Pritzker just said that CBP is only authorized to act within 100
Miles of a border. CBP is saying Lake Michigan is a border as an excuse to invade Chicago
Noting that right now, their definition of the border includes the Great Lakes and Atlantic and Pacific Oceans. That needs to be removed from the area where they are allowed to operate.
its nuts that "within 100 miles of the border" includes the coastlines and shores of the great lakes like i guess we really gotta crack down on atlantisan immigrants
Unfortunately, 65% of the country lives within 100 miles of some border (which includes the oceans and Great Lakes as well as land borders).

It may make sense but it's wrong A 1950s era regulation gave the Border Patrol jurisdiction within 100 miles from the border which includes oceans and the Great Lakes CBP does have some jurisdiction at int'l airports, but the 100 mile rule has never defined the "border" to include airports

But if it's CBP, it begs the question of why they're on the streets doing this over 100 miles from an external boundary to the US where they're limited to be. And you can't say "well, they claim Lake Michigan is an external boundary." You can claim anything but it's still not.
I think it’s 100 miles from any border or coastline including the Great Lakes.

I believe they define it as a land border and argue that because the water at the north end of lake Michigan falls within the border range then the 100 miles starts from shore of all of lake Michigan. I'm frankly surprised they don't then extend it all the way down the Mississippi ...
Didn’t they make a law that stated CPB could operate within 100 miles of any waterway (Great Lakes, ocean?)
Seems like by that logic you could trace every river that connects to the Great Lakes and count everything within 100 miles of those as part of the Border Zone too

Or claiming the flag's gold fringe makes it an Admiralty court, but since it's in Springfield, Illinois, and the center of town is 103 statute miles from the southern edge of Lake Michigan, the charges filed are in the improper venue and invalid. Judges love that one like dogs love floor-chicken!
It's 100 miles, and apparently they start counting at the shore. I imagine there isn't a border checkpoint at the mouth of the lake (which is about 4 miles across).
Border Patrol has jurisdiction 100 miles from water and borders...lake Michigan counts sadly

wait, they're saying Lake Michigan, the only Great Lake 100% within the US? that is very stupid. However, Chicago is completely within the 100 air miles from any external boundary of the U.S. that US Border Patrol claims. www.aclu.org/know-your-ri...

Being as I live in the Great Lakes region in Cleveland, note how they treat that. Chicago is clearly like 500 miles or something from what is shows is the Canadian border, yet they have it and 100 miles inland as border.

CBP/ICE can operate within 100 of any border, coastline or inland waterway that connects to the oceans including the Great Lakes But I still think Minneapolis/St. Paul doesn’t lie in any of those areas. Like everything Trump does, he probably used some bullshit legal argument to do an EO
You have to sail to the diametric opposite end of Lake Michigan to find a border. It’s 400 miles north!

A Squalid Building, a Tip to the Feds, and Then ‘Straight-Up Chaos’ www.nytimes.com/2025/10/19/u... WHAT IS BORDER PATROL DOING IN CHICAGO WHICH IS WELL OVER 100 MILES WITHIN AN INTERNATIONAL BORDER?!! CHICAGO IS OVER 400 MILES FROM BORDER. LAKE MICHIGAN IS 100% WITHIN USA BORDER..!!!😎🇺🇸

Well, you've made me learn something today. Thank you! Turns out, it's not within 100 miles of the actual border, it's within 100 miles of an "external boundary", which includes coasts of any kind. So even though Lake Michigan is entirely within the US, the 100 miles starts at its edge.
How is it even justified for CBP to be in Minneapolis? I thought their jurisdiction was 100 miles from a border, even with the absurd notion that the Lake Michigan shoreline is a “border.” #inners

Why the F is the Coast Guard involved in a raid on a taqueria in Minneapolis? We are over 300 miles from any coastline! If you want to see smuggling over a water route, you'd need to get closer to Duluth by way of Lake Superior. WTF?!?

Also, CBP regards major waterways as "borders" if they terminate in an body of water that has a national border (like, say, an ocean, or Lake Ontario). So e.g. they have been known to assert the right to warrantless search within 100 miles of the Mississippi.

Because they are interconnected, and some of them border Canada, this also includes people who live within 100 miles of one of the Great Lakes. Chicago is not within 100 mi of Canada or one of the oceans, but it's still within 100 miles of the US border.
A bit far for Border Patrol. Their area is supposed to be no more than 100 miles from an entry port. This also includes all the cities on the Great Lakes.

Border patrol has authority 100 miles from functional US boundaries. The Great Lakes are considered boundary, because they are connected and shared with Canada. It’s bullshit, but part of the (complete bullshit) law. Source: ACLU website

1. Vile stuff from CBP
2. It's incredibly disrespectful to the Great Lakes as a whole and especially the Straits of Mackinac for them to consider Chicago within 100 miles of an external border. Get real, man.
I don’t think people realize how far 100 miles is, especially when we’re talking about Great Lakes borders.
Even Chicago is within the 100 miles because they get to use Lake Michigan as the maritime border limit. 😞

.illinois.gov made a great point on .msnbc.com .... Chicago isn't within 100 miles of an external border so why was CBP there? They told him that the state boundary and Lake Michigan is that international boundary... despite the fact that it's still not within 100 miles of Canada

Lake Michigan is. Yes, it's a bullshit power grab. Most of the US population is within 100 miles of a border if you include coastlines, so of course they want to include coastlines, since there are so many refugee boats smuggling people in to Portland and Baltimore and Chicago.

Obviously not the point of this post or map, but I can never get over the fact that they consider Lake Michigan the border ON BOTH SIDES!! Show me how to get to the border in 100 miles of Chicago, or of Gary, Indiana. I'll wait...
I think this is mostly right but have one law of the sea question: Is Chicago with 100 miles of a maritime border? I thought Lake Michigan was US internal waters, like a bay.

Umm, CBP can't operate more than 100 air miles from any US Border. Canada is 300+ miles. And, the nearest Great Lake is 152 miles (Lake Superior). So, using their own bent definitions. By Law, they aren't legally allowed in Minneapolis. But, then again we're talking about the Trump Administration. 😐

The boundary between the Great Lakes and Gulf of Mexico watersheds is within 5 miles of Lake Erie at its narrowest point north of Chautauqua. With a little determined and pointless digging we could be TWO islands.
100 miles is all of MI and people don’t know it. You see ICE in AZ, TX , NM, etc. but don’t expect it in the Great Lakes region. Could happen and there’s nothing we could do.

From Monday on, we can expect US Customs and Border Protection officers to get more active and aggressive, especially in the border zone. That's anywhere within 100 miles of a border, ocean, or one of the Great Lakes. www.aclu.org/know-your-ri...

Federal regulations allow CBP to operate within 100 miles of any US external boundary, which includes US coastlines and international borders. Michigan shares borders with Canada to the north and east, and the Great Lakes to the north, west, and east, all considered "external boundaries".
It's also within about 200 miles of the border which includes all of the great lakes.

you can't use common sense geography: "zone is interpreted as any point of land within 100 miles of land border or within 100 miles of a body of water that is navigable to either mare liberum (“high seas”) or territorial waters of a another country. The latter applies for any of the Great Lakes."

Yes, and if Democrats don't hold firm on these demands then they are complicit as well. These should be the base demands, more should be included like revising the "border" definition to exclude Chicago and other Great Lakes cities that are not really within 100 miles of the border.
Yes, everyone within 100 miles of the Great Lakes is “border,” like Chicago and Milwaukee

They also consider the entirety of the Great Lakes to be borders, which gives them a corridor of 100 miles out from all of them to operate in. Their zone covers every major population center in the country. It’s ridiculous.

But it shows the Dems are standing on business on this. Even if the concession is removal of masks, limiting CBP to within 25 miles of the border and barring the inclusion of the Great Lakes, and limiting ICE access to cities that’s an accomplishment

These morons are clearly lost. Border Patrol is only authorized to operate within 100 miles of the border. Trump has taken his sharpie and ceded Lake Michigan to Canada to allow his masked marauders to invade Illinois. Another of his senile fantasies. www.michiganseagrant.org/topics/great...
I think so? It’s some bullshit like 100 miles inland from the border but I’m not sure where the Canada-US border falls in regards to Lake Michigan
Lake Michigan is not the border. CBP operations in Chicagoland should be banned.

This map is so effing wrong measuring 100 miles that whoever posted it or made it is freaking insane. According to this map Michigan isn't 100 miles across nor a 100 MI deep nor is lake Michigan 100 miles across Believe me you if more than a hundred miles 200 mi across 500 miles north to south

Rachel Maddow is doing an excellent job of reporting the situation in Oregon, and, just now, she interviewed Illinois governor Pritzker, who told her that the brutal Border Patrol agents, who are supposed to stay within 100 miles of a border, are claiming that is the Lake Michigan shoreline.
We do not own Lake Michigan. Border Security can operate within 100 miles of the airport.
I think they can operate here because lake Michigan counts as being a border or something? Idk.

I get what you’re saying but the system was built to be used this way. For instance, CBP has long maintained an understanding of “border” that means Lake Michigan is the “border” and they can operate within 100 miles of that.
Lake Michigan is entirely within the USA so Chicago and Milwaukee are not within 100 miles of Canada border

We also need to use some common sense on defining our borders. Lake Michigan is not a “border” which required CBP in Chicago. Minneapolis needs to really be asking why CBP is there. It’s outside of MSP airport and further than 100 miles from the border. Why isn’t it being questioned?
Why is Border Patrol in Minneapolis? They are not within 100 miles of a border or Lake Superior. They should not be operating in this location.

By law, Customs and Border Patrol's jurisdiction extends from the US border 100 miles inland. Minneapolis is 130 MILES AWAY from the nearest border (Lake Superior), OUTSIDE OF THEIR JURISDICTION. They have NO AUTHORITY to be operating within Minneapolis.
the law is irrelevant because they have no interest in following any law but for context lake superior is approximately 125 miles away. the land border is about 300 miles away

Anyone who lives within 100 miles of any of our borders or the Great Lakes can be asked at any time if they’re a citizen. We aren’t supposed to have to carry proof but they can detain us if they have “reasonable suspicion” which is a lower standard than “probable cause.” Most of us live in this zone
The Great Lakes are water too, there's a border there.

Pritzker said on MSNBC last night that the Border Patrol's excuse for being in Chicago, even though it is more than 100 miles from the border (their jurisdiction), is that Lake Michigan counts as a border. Lake Michigan is the only one of the Great Lakes that doesn't share a border with Canada.
They have jurisdiction for 100 miles from the coasts (which includes the Great Lakes) as well as land borders. That covers well over half the population.
CBP can arrest within 100 miles of the coast. That includes the Great Lakes.

They are allowed a lot of power within 100 miles of a border, which covers A LOT of America and about 2/3rds of the people inside. That includes Everything on the Great Lakes, which obviously includes Chicago.

Also you can't count Lake Michigan as being an external boundary because the boundary exists beyond a point where it touches IL. And even going towards Detroit, it's still outside of 100 miles.

Minnesota is at least a border state. The state makes more sense than when they hit Chicago because Lake Michigan is a “border”. Isn’t CBP limited to 100 miles of a border though? Why are we seeing videos of Bovino in Minneapolis. Why hasn’t any fought the CBP jurisdiction angle there?
But Lake Michigan is entirely us property. Chicago is not within 100 miles of a boarder.
